steps.title: Use the shift shortcut
steps.content: The first five values in data set B are exactly $1$ more than the first five values in data set A: $9=8+1$, $12=11+1$, $13=12+1$, $16=15+1$, and $22=21+1$. Adding the same constant to every value does not change standard deviation.

steps.title: Test II quickly
steps.content: If $h=28$, then the last value of B is also $27+1$. That makes data set B exactly data set A shifted up by $1$, so the standard deviations are equal. The problem needs $\text{SD}(A)>\text{SD}(B)$, so II does not work.

steps.title: Test I and III by spread
steps.content: If $h=23$, the last value of B moves much closer to the center than $28$ would, so data set B is less spread out than data set A. That makes $\text{SD}(A)>\text{SD}(B)$, so I works. If $h=29$, the last value is farther out than the shifted value $28$, so B is more spread out than A and III fails.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Only I could be the value of $h$. That matches choice A.
